results.title: The War is over
results.creator_credit: Rene Bonniere, Malca Gillson and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: Depicts Canadian troops returning home by ship after the first World War and anticipates, in a narrative context, the problems of readjustment they will face centred around questions of authority, discipline, rules and regulations and the effects of war.

results.title: The War game
results.brief_description: Documentary-style story of a nuclear attack on Kent, England. This horrific film is a vision of the possible consequences of a nuclear attack on Britain. Much of the filming was done with a hand-held camera and the result is a film with an impact of remarkable immediacy. An uncompromising film about the latent horrors of the atomic bomb. Note: Not recommended for children.

results.title: The Wannsee Conference
results.creator_credit: Heinz Schirk, Austrian Television-O R F, Bavarian Broadcasting Corporation, Infafilm and Manfred Korytowski
results.brief_description: A real time recreation of the Wannsee Conference, which took place on Tuesday 20th January, 1942, at a house in the quiet Berlin suburb of Wannsee where leading SS and Nazi Party officals gathered to discuss the “Final Solution to the Jewish Question”. Led by SS-General Reinhard Heydrich, the Wannsee Conference was the starting point for the Jewish Holcaust which led to the mass murder of six million people. Actual notes from the meeting, letters written by Hermann Goering and Adolf Eichmann, and testimony by Eichmann at his 1961 trial in Israel were compiled to produce this fascinating historical docudrama. Cast includes Robert Artzhorn, Friedrich Beckhaus, Gerd Bockmann, Jochen Busse, Hans-Werner Bussinger, Dietrich Mattausch. Screenplay by Paul Mommertz. In German with English subtitles.

results.title: The Walls come tumbling down
results.creator_credit: Michael Rubbo, Pierre Lasry, William Weintraub, Roman Bittman and ONF | NFB
results.brief_description: Shows the rampant demolition of older buildings in Montreal to make way for the new glass towers that nobody seems to want. Describes the efforts of individual citizens and citizens’ groups to stem this brutal activity which goes under the name of progress.

results.title: The Waggon train: an exercise in primary drama
results.brief_description: An example of student filmmaking: made of drama work with primary school children at Tanfield Lea School, County Durham. The work was done by a student, Eric Wallace, on teaching practice, for use by the County Durham adviser.

results.title: The Voyeur
results.brief_description: A photographers model (Vivienne Glance) ruminates on her modelling career to come to terms with her changing perception of herself as a female in this role. Her thoughts are punctuated by the images of famous early 20th Century paintings of women by Picasso, Bonnard, Degas and Lautrec along with the voice of a radio narrator (Richard Hawley) who talks of these famous artists and their depiction of women in terms of the male gaze. The female nude is discussed as possession, an object of desire and pawn in a power play and posited against the contemporary to illustrate that very little has changed. Cast includes Darren Williams and Martin Sharp.

results.title: The Voyageurs
results.brief_description: A film about the men who drove big freighter canoes into the wilderness in the days when the fur trade was Canada’s biggest business. The film recreates scenes of the early nineteenth century on the 5 000 km river trade route to the Athabasca. With songs by a male chorus, this film presents a tuneful view of history. The commentary is taken from journals of the time.

results.title: The Voice of the people
results.creator_credit: Ian Allen, Rolf De Heer and AFTRS
results.brief_description: Dramatizes the powerful influence of the television media and argues that it has the ability to misrepresent the opinions of the community through editorial licence. A man’s comments are edited so severely on the evening news that his viewpoint is totally misrepresented and his reaction is shown.

results.title: The Voice of Ivan Frost
results.creator_credit: Alanya Van De Wiel, Ann Shenfield, Paul Fletcher, Robert Stephenson and Victorian College of the Arts
results.brief_description: In a surreal industrial landscape populated by equally bizarre characters, the magical voice of celebrated singer Ivan Frost has the power to make people wish for death. In a remote village elsewhere, Alfonso Pellegrini, a shepherd with an astonishing musical talent, longs to leave his sheep and go in search of the fabulous sounds described in books. Being invited to play beside the remarkable singer, Alfonso is saddened to find himself thrust into the background, his precious violin broken from trying to compete for attention. Ivan’s world is similarly turned upside down when he awakens to find his voice gone and a scar across his chest. Without his legendary voice to bewitch the masses and open doors for him, he is forced to confront the nightmarish reality of his situation - alone.
